introduction.description: Every assessment created on the platform must have one or more reports for specific objectives that serve the test taker.
introduction.key_distinction: The main difference between a profiling report and general report is that the former is based on data from multiple assessments, whereas the latter is based on data from a single assessment.

sub_processes: Define Data Inputs and Report Structure

steps: User clicks 'Generate Report' button
System shows list of Assessments
User selects an assessment
System shows entry fields for Report Title and Report Objective
User enters report title and report objective and submits
System saves inputs and generates unique ID for the report
User clicks 'Add Description' button
System shows entry fields with 'Add Field' and 'Image Upload' facility
User adds text entry/image upload fields, enters content, uploads images and saves
System stores inputs with the newly added Report

types.name: Descriptive Report
types.description: Standard report where candidate scores are presented in tables or graphs and scores are explained against a cut-off.

types.name: Typological Report
types.description: Based on scores, candidates are grouped into different types.

types.name: Interpretative Report
types.description: More detailed, explanatory report where scores are analysed and explained against score ranges defined by score bands.

types.name: Group Report
types.description: Basically used by corporate managers to view their employees' performance on the assessment.

steps: User clicks 'Select Report Type' button
System shows report types
User selects the type of report as per requirement

defining_data_inputs_and_report_structur.description: Inputs from assessment data can be derived from any of the levels: Level0, Level1, Level2, Level3, Level4, and Question level.
defining_data_inputs_and_report_structur.note: Except for Question level data, all other data inputs represent summary scores at their respective levels. Summary scores are NOT retrieved in raw form but in statistically converted form.
steps: User clicks 'Data Inputs' button
System shows data input list: Level0, Level1, Level2, Level3, Level4, Question level
User selects one or more levels
System shows types of converted scores: Percentage, Percentile, STEN, STENINE
User selects converted score type and submits
System saves data input details
data_input_selection_rules: Level0 selected: summary score for entire assessment used
Level1 selected: summary scores of all Level1 variables used
Level2 selected: summary scores of all Level2 variables used
Level3 selected: summary scores of all Level3 variables used
Level4 selected: summary scores of all Level4 variables used
Question Level selected: question scores of all questions used
Multiple levels selected: summary scores belonging to those levels used

steps: User clicks 'Cutoff' button
System shows list of variables as per levels defined and entry fields for: cutoff scores, cutoff label, above-cutoff description, below-cutoff description
User specifies cutoff scores and other details against each variable and saves
System saves cutoff data against each variable
User selects 'Table' or 'Graph' button
System shows entry fields for Table Heads or Graph Legends
User enters relevant text

typological_report.description: Summary scores are ordered in descending order and a specified number of top scoring variables are used to create a personality/intellectual type/profile.

steps: User clicks 'Type Report' button
System shows list of variables and entry fields to specify alphabet/number for Code Generation against each variable
User enters alphabet or number in the CODE column against each variable
System shows entry field to specify number of variables to be drawn for type report
User specifies the number
System shows entry fields where user can define bands and their description for each specified variable
User defines bands and enters descriptive details and submits
System saves the details
type_selection.system_logic: System orders summary scores of all variables in descending order, selects top score variables (as specified by user), applies band details, and generates a Profile Code using the CODE alphabet/number for all selected variables.

sample_output.table_title: TYPE Variable Definition - 3 Highest-score variables, 3 bands

interpretative_report.description: Interpretative report is based on band definition of all variables in the assessment.

steps: User clicks 'Interpretative Report' button
System shows list of variables and entry fields to define bands, labels, and description against each variable
User defines bands and enters other details and submits
System saves inputs

report_layout.description: System generates output values for the report. User defines the layout template.
steps: User clicks 'Report Layout' button
System displays standard report template formats
User selects relevant report template
System presents output values from relevant reports
User defines order of different reports by drag-and-dropping on selected template
System presents text and image insertion functions
User clicks appropriate buttons to insert free text fields or images
System shows preview of the report
User edits the layout if required, or submits to publish

polar_assessment_score_definition.description: Polar assessments have polar variables: same variable is defined as two opposites (e.g., Extroversion vs. Introversion).
polar_assessment_score_definition.rule: Percentage score is used for polar assessments
polar_assessment_score_definition.computation: Opposite variable score = Max Score (100) - Summary Score of the variable
polar_assessment_score_definition.example: If test taker receives 85 on Extroversion-Introversion (predefined as extroversion), then introversion score = 100 - 85 = 15
polar_assessment_score_definition.result: From summary score of each variable, another opposite variable is generated
steps: User clicks 'Define Polar Variables' button
System shows variables from assessment with entry field to define the opposite variable
User enters opposite variable's name and submits
System computes the difference value and assigns it to the opposite variable, and saves
polar_assessment_score_definition.note: If there are six polar assessment variables, including opposite variables there will be 12 variables total.
